name: Bedford Road Medical (Friendly PC) › DBA for Doro Medical
notes:
Now that we're getting our friendly PC off the ground and identifiers assigned, could we get that DBA in?

Here's Cooley's take on DBAs for the PC generally: 

On the DBA, New York requires the DBA to meet the same naming restrictions as the original name, so “Doro” could not be used as part of a DBA. See NYSED “Please be aware that assumed names and dbas must still meet the same naming requirements as original names.”

Telehealth clients that provide services through a “friendly PC” arrangement generally license the MSO’s name to the PC(s). The MSO’s name is used by the PC as the publicly facing brand. The NY PC’s official name is “Bedford Road Medical”, however,  we could use the Doro MSO’s name in publicly facing material through the license granted by the MSO under the management services agreement or another type of licensing agreement. This concept is baked into the Management Agreement we will send.

For example, telehealth company Hims also uses a friendly PC model. The clinical services are provided by their PCs, which do not use the “Hims” name. Instead, the PCs include the following: You Health of Alaska, P.C., You Health of California P.C., P.A., You Health of Hawaii, P.C., You Health of Kansas, P.A., You Health of Nebraska, P.C., You Health Williams of Nevada, P.C., You Health of New Jersey, P.C., and You Health of Texas, P.A. To the public, the PCs are all part of the same “Hims” umbrella. The PCs’ names are generally only included in the terms of use and other legal documents.


And then the NYSED rules:

5. Unless otherwise authorized by law, your application will be rejected if your entity name does not contain the profession being practiced. Regulations of the Commissioner of Education, https://urldefense.com/v3/__https:/www.op.nysed.gov/title8/regulations-commissioner-education/part-59*psc__;Iw!!OPvj_Mo!_zGULyJ8rhSEbEkMVz5cveyfPtMBF7HChCPjYjrTTZ6Ub0SLMKmPCYZ51s4kgCmpJum8LMNfPsC4Olk$.
 
7. Your application will be rejected if you do not submit an explanation for words used in your entity name that are: initials, foreign words, surnames, abbreviations or words not commonly used in connection with the profession(s) being practiced (https://urldefense.com/v3/__http:/public.leginfo.state.ny.us/LAWSSEAF.cgi?QUERYTYPE=LAWS*&QUERYDATA=$$BSC1503$$@TXBSC01503*&LIST=LAW*&BROWSER=EXPLORER*&TOKEN=27738319*&TARGET=VIEW__;KysrKys!!OPvj_Mo!_zGULyJ8rhSEbEkMVz5cveyfPtMBF7HChCPjYjrTTZ6Ub0SLMKmPCYZ51s4kgCmpJum8LMNfMwheFmU$; https://urldefense.com/v3/__https:/www.op.nysed.gov/title8/regulations-commissioner-education/part-59*psc__;Iw!!OPvj_Mo!_zGULyJ8rhSEbEkMVz5cveyfPtMBF7HChCPjYjrTTZ6Ub0SLMKmPCYZ51s4kgCmpJum8LMNfPsC4Olk$; https://urldefense.com/v3/__https:/www.op.nysed.gov/title8/rules-board-regents/part-29__;!!OPvj_Mo!_zGULyJ8rhSEbEkMVz5cveyfPtMBF7HChCPjYjrTTZ6Ub0SLMKmPCYZ51s4kgCmpJum8LMNfKf3srB8$). name: Call Ben
Claude BAA
notes:
Short summary of next steps: 
    Get an org API key. We use that to authenticate instead of Claude Teams
    Maria sets up zero data retention (ZDR)
    We sign a BAA

Gist of the conversation is was that they want this to be easier, and are working to make it easier. Right now there are some parts of their system which are easy to cover with a BAA, and parts that take more manual work on their side, which are the ones that need an enterprise account to get BAA coverage. Maria hinted that that might change quite soon, but didn't give a date. 

What was in vs out was a bit hard to follow. Maria said she'd send it in a follow-up email. What was clear was that Claude Cowork and Claude Code UI were hard, while API and CLI Claude Code, authenticated with an API key, were easy.

From: Kathryn Beilke <mail@networkforgood.com>
To: hello@doromind.com

Dear Doro Mind,

This Mental Health Awareness Month, we are reminded of how fortunate we are to do our work here in New York State, which is ranked first in the nation by http://eml.networkforgood.com/ls/click?upn=u001.I07pyPwPPDQb6h0RiBx2u7-2BdBNOSgC-2Ffe66Mm15epfDKzi4xSh83hKC1AaG6cR7Z8an0NY3tZ9jw6On46gdKU-2BlgipL3iQa6hiIZAmhkyYWsWtKDbmOz785WRR1CD-2FifBrP6wVFw-2F-2Bye3AbikDk8CQ-3D-3DezN8_xdUTvUhIjWlSJgufBblll4oT-2FQkz-2Bxhhw2CmtnbWinYv-2BTlMV-2Bdsr2GyD8MjR9R6Qg6bs3yfAGpkNctxgJ7q3k0sfQsyJdJmYQ9X1-2B596pIT38SEDzqwMI0Nd-2F7NydL6D4zshJcrPmvF1i7X1X8rJECYEwWdPa73aRP7EalX-2Bw3-2FiEQk3oBdfrq976h4-2Flf42OEmWdsJrXhzBKmtNSwT86sLFzT3n4-2FjIvmy-2FTvuLfeoDItynV0zPGD0Bj8SezXY08sUyvG5fcF1fLSf0jCxYMiSofFsgGTXnsUC1OuiLAw9qimCBFpPDT1hROeLP-2FIpCe7Up2jGWfp1T7j6s2gr5EC2NoHXeVo5I3PIsT-2FAOMQ-3D – a validation of the efficacy of New York’s multi-billion dollar investment in Mental Health services over the last few years. 

But we still have a long way to go. This week, NAMI NYS convened our NAMI NEXTGen leaders from across the state at the New York State Capitol where we witnessed something truly powerful. 

These young advocates shared their lived experiences with extraordinary courage — speaking directly with legislators, addressing a press conference, and championing a robust legislative agenda that would strengthen mental health across our state. Their candor and determination were inspiring. And it reminded everyone in the room that while mental health is deeply personal, systemic support is necessary for all our work at NAMI across New York State. 

At NAMI NYS, we are committed to centering voices like these incredible young people to ensure that New York continues to forge ahead in its leadership. Your support makes it possible for us to advocate that every New Yorker can access compassionate, effective, and equitable mental health care and support. 

Please consider making a gift today to enable us to continue to ensure your voice is heard in the halls of power in New York! 

In gratitude,

Kathryn Beilke

Development Manager

NAMI New York State

**License:** Hannah Bock-Brownstein | RN, Maryland (Permanent)
**Requirement:** CBC
**Details:** You are required to do a (https://www.dpscs.state.md.us/publicservs/bgchecks.shtml) criminal background check before applying for your RN/LPN Endorsement and Temporary License.  A twelve-digit CJIS (Criminal Justice Information System) Tracking Number will be assigned to you after you initiate your background check process.  You may obtain the CJIS Tracking Number by contacting the Maryland Department of Public Safety and Correctional Services directly (https://www.dpscs.state.md.us/publicservs/fingerprint.shtml) (view a report sample), or through use of an online checking system available in many local police stations and approved fingerprinting facilities (view a report sample).  If you are an out-of-state resident and need a fingerprint card for Maryland, please send an e-mail to the Maryland Board of Nursing at mdbon.nets@maryland.gov to get a fingerprint card (view a card sample).  

Please share your twelve-digit CJIS Tracking Number below after completion
